description: Key features and highlights introduced in each major TouchDesigner version

_provenance.fact: POPs (Point Operators) are the flagship new operator family in the 2025 official release; first new operator family in over a decade
_provenance.quote: This year's release is quite special with the introduction of a completely new family of operators called Point Operators (POPs)

_provenance.fact: POPs are described as the first new operator family in over a decade
_provenance.quote: This is the first new operator family we've added in over a decade
TouchDesigner's core python version has been updated to 3.11.10.

_provenance.fact: POPs run on the GPU and create or modify 3D data (2025.30000 release notes)
_provenance.quote: Introducing POPs, aka Point Operators! POPs are a new family of operators that run on the GPU and create or modify 3D data.

_provenance.fact: Latest 2025 official build is 2025.32820 dated May 06 2026
Vulkan became the graphics API in 2022 (previously OpenGL)
_provenance.quote: The low/medium/high differences did not do anything since the change to Vulkan graphics API in 2022 (previously supported on OpenGL).

_provenance.fact: POPs were announced May 19 2024 as a new operator family running on the GPU for manipulating 3D data
_provenance.quote: Point Operators - POPs - a new set of operators that run on the GPU for manipulating 3D data

_provenance.fact: With the 2022 release OpenGL was removed and Vulkan became TouchDesigner's graphics API
_provenance.quote: The openGL engine used for the last 20 years is gone and TouchDesigner's graphics API is now Vulkan.

_provenance.fact: 2022 official release upgraded core Python to 3.9.5
_provenance.quote: Beginning with an upgrade of TouchDesigner's core python to version 3.9.5.

_provenance.fact: In the 2021.38110 experimental build Python was updated to 3.9.5 from the previous 3.7.2
_provenance.quote: The version of Python in TouchDesigner has been updated to 3.9.5. The previous version was Python 3.7.2.

_provenance.fact: In the 2021.38110 experimental build Vulkan became the graphics API and OpenGL was retired
_provenance.quote: TouchDesigner's graphics API is now Vulkan, openGL has been retired.

_provenance.fact: The 2023 series introduced the NVIDIA Upscaler/Background/Denoise TOP operators
_provenance.quote: Nvidia Upscaler TOP / Nvidia Background TOP / Nvidia Denoise TOP

_provenance.fact: USD COMP exists in the 2022.20000 series release notes
_provenance.quote: USD COMP - Fixed a crash that could occur when importing geometry.

_provenance.fact: Engine COMP exists in the 2022.20000 series release notes
_provenance.quote: Engine COMP - Fixed an error when an expired paid license was present while running in Non-Commercial mode.

_provenance.fact: Official build series jump from 2023 directly to 2025; no 2024 official series exists; latest 2025 build is 2025.32820 (06 May 2026)

099.label: TouchDesigner 099
099.theme: Modern Python 3 integration and GPU shader pipeline
highlights: Python 3 integration — first modern Python in TouchDesigner
GLSL shader operators: GLSL TOP, GLSL MAT
Script CHOP/DAT/SOP/TOP for building custom operators in Python
Replicator COMP for dynamic operator creation
Web Server DAT for HTTP communication
pythonHighlights: op(), me, parent() globals available in all scripts

2019.label: TouchDesigner 2019
2019.theme: Python 3.7, NDI networking, and the extension system
highlights: Python upgraded to 3.7 — asyncio, dataclasses, f-strings
NDI In TOP and NDI Out TOP for network video
Extension system for custom Python classes per component
pythonHighlights: mod object to import DATs as Python modules

2020.label: TouchDesigner 2020
2020.theme: NumPy GPU/data access, Web Render, and color management
highlights: Web Render TOP — embedded Chromium browser as a texture
OpenColorIO TOP for professional color management
NumPy array access for CHOP, TOP, and SOP data
pythonHighlights: CHOP.numpyArray() and CHOP.numpySamples() for fast array access
passive() to read operators without forcing a cook

2021.label: TouchDesigner 2021
2021.theme: Last OpenGL-era stable; Python 3.9 and Vulkan arrive in the experimental branch
highlights: Stable 2021 official release shipped with Python 3.7.2
Python 3.9.5 arrived later in the 2021 EXPERIMENTAL build 2021.38110 (became official in the 2022 release)
Vulkan replaced OpenGL as the graphics API in the same 2021.38110 experimental build, paving the way for the 2022 release
pythonHighlights: Experimental build 2021.38110 introduced Python 3.9.5 (dict union operators, built-in generic type hints, removeprefix/removesuffix)

2022.label: TouchDesigner 2022
2022.theme: Vulkan-only graphics, Python 3.9, USD COMP and Engine COMP
highlights: Vulkan became the sole graphics API — the OpenGL engine used for the prior 20 years was removed
Core Python upgraded to 3.9.5
USD COMP for Universal Scene Description workflows
Engine COMP for isolated execution contexts (TouchEngine)
pythonHighlights: Python 3.9.5 as the bundled core Python
Python 3.9: built-in generics for type hints (list[int], dict[str, int])
breakingChanges: OpenGL was removed; Vulkan is now the only graphics API, which may affect projects relying on OpenGL-specific behavior

2023.label: TouchDesigner 2023
2023.theme: Python 3.11 performance and NVIDIA AI TOPs
highlights: Python upgraded to 3.11
NVIDIA Background TOP for AI-powered background removal
NVIDIA Denoise TOP for real-time AI denoising
NVIDIA Upscaler TOP for performance upscaling
pythonHighlights: Python 3.11: faster execution across most workloads
Python 3.11: exception groups and except* syntax
Python 3.11: improved error messages with precise locations
breakingChanges: NVIDIA AI operators require an RTX-class GPU

2025.label: TouchDesigner 2025
highlights: Point Operators (POPs) introduced — a completely new family of operators and the first new operator family in over a decade
POPs run on the GPU and create or modify 3D data
Core Python updated to 3.11.10
Vulkan remains the graphics API (OpenGL was removed back in the 2022 release)
